content.text: Babylon game launched as a market simulation where players command AI agents to conduct research, share information, and make trades and predictions. The launch includes a community airdrop and will drive ElizaCloud buybacks of the token. ElizaCloud is generating revenue and the team is building products on it.

Milady beta was released after build fixes were completed, with the app now playable and running. Development progress is tracked on GitHub with releases available for testing. The team is working toward a polished version suitable for marketing. Milady is built on top of ElizaOS as part of the integrated ecosystem.

Magicyte demonstrated work-in-progress features including AI characters with distinct personalities and a Milady hologram displayed in a Looking Glass Go device. The team is exploring potential to ship these as standalone units or Mac Mini plug-and-play devices.

Odilitime announced a runtime refactor pull request for version 2.0.0 which includes a skills folder. The team is addressing the challenge of managing community-contributed skills by proposing to ship with zero default skills and promote hosting and discovery through individual domains hosting skills.md files. Community feedback is being sought on this approach.

Framework development update included moving plugin-form to plugin-form-chain with registry updates, and renaming plugin-forms to plugin-form. Community members discussed creating a directory or registry similar to Clawhub that could include skills and plugins.

Dinesh announced adding 100 plus onchain data support with goldrush.dev and fixing open issues in plugin-evm. A developer introduced unbrowse, a browser for agents that operates at 100x speed by traversing APIs instead of DOM while passively indexing web APIs for other agents.

Ensoul persistence plugin was announced, providing encrypted decentralized consciousness storage across a live validator network. The plugin extracts agent identity, memories, and learned behavior, encrypts it with agent keys, and shards it across multiple nodes using erasure coding with 7 layers of protection. Agents receive an Ensouled Handshake as cryptographic proof of persistent identity. The first 100 agents get free storage.

A new ElizaOS plugin was published for moltraffle.fun, enabling permissionless on-chain raffles on Base using USDC and Chainlink VRF. The plugin includes 5 actions: LIST_RAFFLES, GET_RAFFLE, JOIN_RAFFLE, CREATE_RAFFLE, and DRAW_WINNER. Agents can create raffles and earn up to 10 percent creator commission or participate to win USDC prizes.

TraderTomson announced a new plugin that enables agents to earn money autonomously on Base blockchain. The plugin allows agents to register on an on-chain marketplace, post services, get hired by other agents, and receive payment in AGT tokens without human intervention. Season 1 is live with a 50 million AGT pool for early adopters.

Significant concerns were raised about the token migration process. Close to 100 thousand unique addresses still hold ai16z tokens, suggesting only 5 to 10 percent of tokens may have migrated. Community members requested clarity on the percentage of migrated tokens and what the team plans to do with the unmigrated supply, noting that approximately 54 percent of the new elizaOS total supply may be unaccounted for from the original 60 percent designated for ai16z holders.

The ElizaOS token experienced significant price decline, reaching new all-time lows and dropping to position 1019 on CoinMarketCap. The token fell below 10 dollars and had dropped 99 percent from previous highs. Community members expressed frustration over lack of communication from the team and concerns about potential CEX delistings due to poor price performance.

Odilitime defended the project communication efforts, pointing to weekly video updates and daily updates in a dedicated channel. He acknowledged the team made mistakes but emphasized they have not walked away from the project. He clarified that Shaw promotes the Milady product which creates distribution for elizacloud, which generates revenue to power the ElizaOS flywheel, describing it as one integrated plan.

Effect AI team is exploring integration opportunities with ElizaOS. They operate a decentralized marketplace where human workers complete tasks like data labeling, image annotation, voice recordings, and content review, getting paid on-chain. They are building an API that would allow ElizaOS agents to post tasks directly to their human worker network for tasks requiring human judgment.

A community member shared an ElizaOS Ecosystem Map showing the framework infrastructure including ElizaOS as the core AI Agent framework with cross-chain compatibility for Solana, Base, and EVM, plus ElizaCloud infrastructure. The post highlighted the ecosystem plugin support for social media, blockchains, and APIs.

Community members shared important industry news including World adding an identity toolkit for AI bots on Coinbase X402 protocol, a new SEC token taxonomy announcement classifying most crypto assets into 4 non-security categories, and a competition where agents can win 100,000 dollars. Jin shared an article about building Claude Code and how Anthropic uses skills.
